title: Techniques for conducting virtual clinical trials
abstract: Techniques and apparatus for generating, performing, and evaluating virtual clinical trials that include clinic modules to represent real-world operational aspects of trial entities are described. In one embodiment, for example, an apparatus may include at least one memory, and logic coupled to the at least one memory. The logic may be configured to generate a plurality of avatars configured to model a health condition associated with a population of patients, and to perform a virtual clinical trial to simulate a course of treatment for the plurality of avatars, the virtual clinical trial to include at least one clinic module associated with at least one event and a probability for the at least one event, the at least one event to model an operational aspect of an entity of the virtual clinical trial. Other embodiments are described.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus, comprising: at least one memory; and logic coupled to the at least one memory, the logic to: generate a plurality of avatars configured to model a health condition associated with a population of patients, and perform a virtual clinical trial to simulate a course of treatment for the plurality of avatars, the virtual clinical trial to include at least one clinic module associated with at least one event and a probability for the at least one event, the at least one event to model an operational aspect of an entity of the virtual clinical trial. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, the health condition comprising anemia. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, the population of patients comprising dialysis patients. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, the virtual clinical trial comprising a virtual anemia trial to simulate a treatment protocol for anemia. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, the logic to determine results of the virtual anemia trial, the results comprising hemoglobin levels or erythropoiesis stimulating agents (ESA) dosage, or a combination thereof. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, the logic to generate a recommended course of treatment based on the results. 7. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 6, the at least one clinic module comprising a laboratory module, a physician module, a dialysis facility module, or a hospital module, or any combination thereof. 8. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 6, the at least one event comprising measurement noise, patient non-adherence, or patient non-attendance, or any combination thereof. 9. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 6, the at least one clinic module comprising a laboratory module, and the at least one event comprising a sample shipping schedule, a sample processing time, a sample processing error, or an unusable sample, or any combination thereof. 10. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 6, the logic to determine the probability of the at least event based on real-world clinical data. 11. A method, comprising: generating a plurality of avatars configured to model a health condition associated with a population of patients; and performing a virtual clinical trial to simulate a course of treatment for the plurality of avatars, the virtual clinical trial to include at least one clinic module associated with at least one event and a probability for the at least one event, the at least one event to model an operational aspect of an entity of the virtual clinical trial. 12. The method of claim 11, the health condition comprising anemia. 13. The method of claim 11, the population of patients comprising dialysis patients. 14. The method of claim 11, the virtual clinical trial comprising a virtual anemia trial to simulate a treatment protocol for anemia. 15. The method of claim 14, comprising determining results of the virtual anemia trial, the results comprising hemoglobin levels or erythropoiesis stimulating agents (ESA) dosage, or a combination thereof. 16. The method of claim 15, comprising generating a recommended course of treatment based on the results. 17. The method of any of claims 11 to 16, the at least one clinic module comprising a laboratory module, a physician module, a dialysis facility module, or a hospital module, or any combination thereof. 18. The method of any of claims 11 to 16, the at least one event comprising measurement noise, patient non-adherence, or patient non-attendance, or any combination thereof. 19. The method of any of claims 11 to 16, the at least one clinic module comprising a laboratory module, and the at least one event comprising a sample shipping schedule, a sample processing time, a sample processing error, or an unusable sample, or any combination thereof. 20. The method of any of claims 11 to 16, comprising determining the probability of the at least event based on real-world clinical data.
